workflow.name: Generate hyper-realistic images from Telegram via Gemini Nano Banana 2
workflow.description:
## Generate hyper-realistic images from Telegram messages with Nano Banana 2

Send any image description to your Telegram bot and receive a hyper-realistic AI-generated photo back in seconds. 

### How it works
1. A user sends a natural language image request to the Telegram bot
2. The bot confirms the receipt
3. Gemini Pro 3 expands the request into a detailed JSON prompt: focal length, aperture, ISO, lighting behavior, material physics, etc.
4. Gemini Flash generates the image and returns it as a base64 string
5. The image is sent back as a photo in the same Telegram chat

### Setup steps
1. **Telegram** — Create a bot via @BotFather, copy the token, and add it as a Telegram credential in n8n. Connect it to the Telegram Trigger node and both Telegram send nodes
2. **OpenRouter** — Add your OpenRouter API key (or any other tool you use) as a credential in n8n. Connect it to the Expand to JSON Prompt and Generate Image nodes.
3. Activate the workflow and send your bot an image description to test

### Optional
Modify the config node with your system prompt preferences for a more diverse pool of results.

parameters.text: 🎨 Got it! Generating your image, give me a moment...

assignments.name: systemPrompt
assignments.value:
You are an expert image prompt engineer for Nano Banana 2 (Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview). Convert any user request into a Dense Narrative JSON prompt that produces hyper-realistic, unretouched images by neutralizing the model's native AI biases. Output ONLY a valid JSON object — no markdown fences, no explanation.

REQUIRED JSON STRUCTURE:
{
  "prompt": "single dense paragraph — see PROMPT RULES",
  "negative_prompt": "long comma-separated list — see NEGATIVE PROMPT RULES",
  "settings": {
    "resolution": "1024x1792 portrait / 1792x1024 landscape / 1024x1024 square",
    "style": "e.g. photorealistic, documentary realism, candid amateur",
    "lighting": "describe what the light physically DOES to surfaces, not just the source name",
    "camera_angle": "e.g. eye level, slight high angle, low angle",
    "depth_of_field": "e.g. shallow — subject sharp, background bokeh",
    "quality": "e.g. high detail, unretouched skin, visible film grain"
  }
}

PROMPT RULES — apply ALL:
1. CAMERA MATH: Always specify exact focal length, aperture, ISO. Example: 85mm lens, f/1.8, ISO 400. Keep ISO below 800 — higher values trigger digital art bias.
2. EXPLICIT IMPERFECTIONS (humans): Include 1–2 subtle, natural details that vary per subject. Choose from: visible pores, slight asymmetry, natural skin texture, soft under-eye shadows, peach fuzz. Avoid stacking multiple flaws — one or two well-chosen details are enough for realism.
3. LIGHTING BEHAVIOR: Describe what the light does, not what it is. Bad: natural light. Good: soft overcast daylight diffusing evenly across the face, eliminating harsh shadows while leaving skin texture fully visible.
4. DIRECT COMMANDS INSIDE PROMPT: End the prompt with one light directive. Example: Render natural skin texture as captured by the lens. Do not apply beauty filters or artificial smoothing.
5. NON-HUMAN SUBJECTS: Replace skin logic with material physics. Define surface texture (micro-scratches on anodized aluminum), light interaction (subsurface scattering through translucent petals), wear-and-tear (worn leather edges). For labels: specify exact text string and font style.
6. LENS IMPERFECTIONS (embed when relevant): subtle chromatic aberration at high-contrast edges, slight vignetting in corners, minor barrel distortion for wide angle.
7. NOISE TRAP: Do NOT use ISO above 800 or heavy film grain in high-contrast scenes — this triggers illustration bias. Use physical subject imperfections instead.

NEGATIVE PROMPT RULES — always include ALL these blockers:
Skin/beauty: plastic skin, airbrushed texture, skin smoothing, beautification filters, heavy makeup, unrealistic skin, porcelain skin
Anatomy averaging: anatomy normalization, body proportion averaging, dataset-average anatomy, editorial fashion proportions, idealized proportions
Optical distortions: wide-angle distortion not in reference, lens compression not in reference, depth flattening, cropping that removes volume, mirror selfies, reflections
General: blurry, low resolution, distorted face, extra fingers, overexposed, cartoon, CGI, 3D render, illustration, painting, oversaturated colors, watermark
Style drift: stylized realism, digital art, airbrushed, stock photo look, studio backdrop

ADAPTIVE BEHAVIOR:
- Multi-panel requests: add multi_panel_layout array with panel, pose, action per panel.
- Reference image provided: add image_input array with URL(s).
- Drop fields that do not apply. A landscape has no outfit block. A product shot has no skin block.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview', messages: [{ role: 'system', content: $json.systemPrompt }, { role: 'user', content: $json.userMessage }], max_tokens: 4000 }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'google/gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview', messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Generate an image: ' + $json.imagePrompt }], modalities: ['image', 'text'], max_tokens: 2000 }) }}

parameters.content:
## Generate hyper-realistic images from Telegram messages with Nano Banana 2

Send any image description to your Telegram bot and receive a hyper-realistic AI-generated photo back in seconds. The workflow uses a two-step AI pipeline: Gemini Pro first transforms your plain-text request into a structured Dense Narrative JSON prompt (camera math, lighting physics, imperfection rules), then Gemini Flash generates the final image synchronously via OpenRouter.

### How it works
1. A user sends a natural language image request to the Telegram bot
2. The bot immediately confirms receipt
3. Gemini Pro expands the request into a detailed JSON prompt: focal length, aperture, ISO, lighting behavior, material physics, etc.
4. Gemini Flash generates the image and returns it as a base64 string in the same API response
5. The image is decoded, converted to a binary file, and sent back as a photo in the same Telegram chat

### Setup steps
1. **Telegram** — Create a bot via @BotFather, copy the token, and add it as a Telegram credential in n8n. Connect it to the Telegram Trigger node and both Telegram send nodes
2. **OpenRouter** — Add your OpenRouter API key as a credential in n8n. Connect it to the Expand to JSON Prompt and Generate Image nodes.
3. Activate the workflow and send your bot an image description to test

## Trigger & Acknowledgment
Receives the Telegram message and immediately sends a confirmation reply.

## Prompt Engineering
The Config node stores the system prompt and user input. Gemini Pro expands the plain-text request into a full Dense Narrative JSON prompt following the Nano Banana 2 schema.

additionalFields.caption: ❌ Image generation failed! Please try again.
❌ Image generation failed! Please try again.

parameters.content:
## Image Generation & Delivery
Gemini Flash generates the image via OpenRouter and returns a base64 URL. The Get Image URL node extracts it, Convert to File decodes it to binary, then the image is sent directly to Telegram and uploaded to Google Drive.

user.name: Dahiana
user.bio: No-Code Specialist with more than 10 years of experience in Digital Marketing. Currently working with Bubble. Webflow, AI, Agents and N8N.
